Title: Creating yellow seed Camelina sativa with enhanced oil accumulation by CRISPR-mediated disruption of Transparent Testa 8

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: bHLH transcription factor with N-terminal MYB interaction region (MIR) and C-terminal bHLH domain
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: seed oil storage and biofuel feedstock value; flavonoids contribute seed coat pigmentation
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss of function of CsTT8-2 together with the other two CsTT8 homeologs reduces seed flavonoid accumulation, causes yellow seed coat, reduces mucilage, and increases seed total fatty acid and TAG accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CsTT8-2 is one of three seed-expressed TT8 homeologs in camelina. 2. Triple disruption of CsTT8 homeologs caused yellow seeds with 35.5–44.1% lower flavonoid content and ~80% lower mucilage. 3. Triple mutants showed increased fatty acid synthesis rate and increased seed TFA from 32.4% to as high as 38.0% DW and TAG yield by >21%.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: CRISPR/Cas9 knockout editing; genotyping by Sanger sequencing; RT-qPCR; RNA-seq expression profiling; flavonoid quantification; mucilage quantification; GC-MS/GC fatty acid and TAG profiling; [14C]acetate incorporation assay
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly expressed in developing seeds, peaking around 10–12 DAF; much lower in roots and barely detectable in leaves and stems
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Highly expressed in developing seeds, peaking around 10–12 DAF
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Developing seeds at 10–24 DAF under growth chamber conditions
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: seed coat flavonoid accumulation; seed mucilage formation; fatty acid biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: LEC1; LEC2; FUS3; WRI1; CT(alpha-CT/CT); BCCP1; BCCP2; KASI; KASII; KASIII; FAD2; FAD3; possibly GL2; TTG2
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Disruption of CsTT8 increased expression of LEC1, LEC2, FUS3, WRI1 and several fatty acid synthesis genes; FAD2 increased and FAD3 slightly decreased; editing may decrease GL2 and TTG2 expression
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: negative regulator whose removal substantially reprograms seed metabolism toward higher oil accumulation and lower flavonoid deposition
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Likely component of the MBW (MYB-bHLH-WD40) complex; editing may disrupt MBW complex formation
Regulation_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: MYB partners (MBW complex, not individually validated in this paper); TTG1-complex context
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: All three CsTT8 homeologs act redundantly for seed coat color; mutation of one or two isoforms was insufficient to produce clear yellow seeds. CsTT8-8 showed the highest expression among homeologs. Effects were demonstrated for triple mutants rather than single-gene mutants alone.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Slight decrease in hundred-seed weight in two of three triple-mutant lines; no major change in starch or protein content; no obvious growth defects
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Excellent gene-editing target for generating yellow-seeded, higher-oil camelina germplasm; can be stacked with other oil-improving traits for biofuel and lipid bioproduct production

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: bHLH transcription factor with N-terminal MYB interaction region (MIR) and C-terminal bHLH domain
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: seed oil storage and biofuel feedstock value; flavonoids contribute seed coat pigmentation
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss of function of CsTT8-8 together with the other two CsTT8 homeologs reduces seed flavonoid accumulation, causes yellow seed coat, reduces mucilage, and increases seed total fatty acid and TAG accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CsTT8-8 is the most highly expressed TT8 homeolog during seed development. 2. Triple disruption of CsTT8 homeologs caused yellow seeds with markedly reduced flavonoids and mucilage. 3. Triple mutants showed increased fatty acid synthesis rate and increased seed oil accumulation.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: CRISPR/Cas9 knockout editing; genotyping by Sanger sequencing; RT-qPCR; RNA-seq expression profiling; flavonoid quantification; mucilage quantification; GC-MS/GC fatty acid and TAG profiling; [14C]acetate incorporation assay
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly expressed in developing seeds, peaking around 10–12 DAF; highest among the three homeologs; much lower in roots and barely detectable in leaves and stems
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Highly expressed in developing seeds, peaking around 10–12 DAF
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Developing seeds at 10–24 DAF under growth chamber conditions
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: seed coat flavonoid accumulation; seed mucilage formation; fatty acid biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: LEC1; LEC2; FUS3; WRI1; CT(alpha-CT/CT); BCCP1; BCCP2; KASI; KASII; KASIII; FAD2; FAD3; possibly GL2; TTG2
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Disruption of CsTT8 increased expression of LEC1, LEC2, FUS3, WRI1 and several fatty acid synthesis genes; FAD2 increased and FAD3 slightly decreased; editing may decrease GL2 and TTG2 expression
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: negative regulator whose removal substantially reprograms seed metabolism toward higher oil accumulation and lower flavonoid deposition
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Likely component of the MBW (MYB-bHLH-WD40) complex; editing may disrupt MBW complex formation
Regulation_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: MYB partners (MBW complex, not individually validated in this paper); TTG1-complex context
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Most highly expressed homeolog, but phenotype still required disruption of all three CsTT8 genes, indicating functional redundancy.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Slight decrease in hundred-seed weight in two of three triple-mutant lines; no major change in starch or protein content; no obvious growth defects
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Excellent gene-editing target for generating yellow-seeded, higher-oil camelina germplasm; can be stacked with other oil-improving traits for biofuel and lipid bioproduct production
